headline: Kenyir Lake Algal Blooms Threaten RM240M Freshwater Fisheries
context: Terengganu state fisheries reported persistent algal blooms in Kenyir Lake since 2023, threatening RM240 million in freshwater fisheries and 12,000 fishing-dependent livelihoods.

cards.big: Kenyir Lake is Southeast Asia's largest man-made freshwater lake.
cards.sub: Persistent algal blooms since 2023 have killed fish in 4 zones and closed 8 fishing areas.

cards.big: Nutrient loading from 18 upstream farms is the primary bloom trigger.
cards.sub: Agricultural runoff carries nitrogen and phosphorus that fuel algae growth.

cards.big: 12,000 people depend on Kenyir fisheries for their primary income.
cards.sub: Average income for affected fishermen dropped RM800 per month since blooms began.

cards.big: Algal bloom remediation costs RM4.5M annually.
cards.sub: Upstream agricultural best practices would cost RM1.2M but require multi-agency coordination.

cards.big: Spending RM4.5M to treat blooms caused by RM1.2M in preventable runoff is paying 4 times more to solve than to prevent.
Lake ecosystems need catchment-level management. Treating the lake while ignoring upstream farms is like treating symptoms while feeding the disease.
